subject: Ecology
title: Convergent Evolution on Islands
description: A quick win: understand Convergent Evolution on Islands.

hook.text: Unrelated species on different islands often evolve strikingly similar solutions.

buildup.text: Anole lizards on different Caribbean islands evolved the same set of body types independently.

discovery.text: Each island produced a twig anole, a trunk anole, and a canopy anole—same niches, same shapes.

twist.text: Hawaiian silverswords and African lobelias evolved similar rosette forms on separate volcanoes.

climax.text: Convergence proves that ecology, not ancestry, drives form when niches are similar.

punchline.text: Same problem, same solution, different ancestors.

quiz.question: What does convergent evolution on islands demonstrate?
options: All islands share the same species
